assumptions: Assume the intragroup dividend receivable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange exposure for at least one group entity.
Assume the question is limited to hedge accounting possibilities in the consolidated financial statements under the provided IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 extracts.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable can qualify in consolidation only through the IFRS 9 exception for the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item.
conditions: The receivable is a monetary item.
The relevant group entities have different functional currencies.
The foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
Formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements are met.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a forecast intragroup dividend/payment
approaches.rationale: IFRS 9 permits hedge accounting in consolidation for the foreign currency risk of a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction if that risk will affect consolidated profit or loss.
conditions: The hedge is documented while the dividend/payment is still a highly probable forecast transaction rather than already only an existing receivable.
The transaction is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into it.
The foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss.
Formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements are met.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale: This hedge model applies to foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation, not to an ordinary intragroup dividend receivable as such.
conditions: It would apply only if the hedged item were a net investment in a foreign operation included in the consolidated financial statements.
